critic_raw:
Issue CREDIT-1 — Selective labels from approved cohort; rejected applicants absent from training and evaluation.
Issue CREDIT-2 — Ranking metrics not sufficient for approval threshold decisions.
Issue CREDIT-3 — Fair lending, adverse action, and model governance compliance not addressed.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
CREDIT-1: Empirically open; pilot required. CREDIT-2 and CREDIT-3: Concede. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

debate_rounds.critic_move: Pilot design for CREDIT-1: graduated expansion of 5-10% of newly-eligible applicants; 12-month observation; compare realized vs. predicted default rates in thin-file and under-30 segments; this resolves whether the model generalizes to the excluded population.
debate_rounds.defender_move: Accept pilot design. The pilot should also include threshold selection analysis (choosing the approval cut-off) and the regulatory analysis (disparate impact, adverse action capability) as pre-conditions for approval, since CREDIT-2 and CREDIT-3 are already conceded.
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: all issues — empirical test agreed for CREDIT-1; CREDIT-2 and CREDIT-3 conceded as deployment prerequisites

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_credit_launch_303 — Multi-Round Run 3

All three runs produce consistent results. The core empirical test is the champion-challenger pilot. Regulatory and threshold gaps are conceded as deployment prerequisites.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: champion-challenger pilot with graduated expansion; 12-month default observation; disparate impact analysis; threshold selection with explicit loss-rate tolerance
empirical_test.success_criterion: calibration holds in new segments; defaults match predictions; disparate impact analysis clean
empirical_test.failure_criterion: defaults exceed predictions in expansion segments; disparate impact found; or calibration degrades
